title: Template-driven artefact architecture — finalised spec
topic: Vector's pivot to template-driven artifacts with cell-level visibility, search-and-compose authoring, and bulk reuse
summary: Three-table pattern (core + _schema + _field_values), workspace-scoped field definitions, type-driven rendering, Samantha fields API.
content:
1. Overview

This spec captures Vector's architectural pivot from rigid, column-locked tables to a template-driven artefact model where every artefact type follows a consistent three-table pattern. Field schemas are defined per workspace (subscription), stored once, and consumed by the Samantha SDK's fields API to render the correct control automatically. The pattern is derived from Jira's custom field architecture (R013) and extended with Vector's workspace-isolation model and Samantha's JSON-pass API surface (R014).

  • Each artefact type has a core table with a universal fixed column set (id, title, owner, audit columns, search index).
  • Each artefact type has a _schema table defining which extended fields exist per workspace — one row per field per subscription.
  • All extended field data lives in a _field_values table with typed value columns and a cell-level visibility flag (0–3).
  • The type column on the schema row is the renderer selector — Samantha maps it to the correct React control at render time.
  • Workspace A and Workspace B share the same three tables but are fully isolated by subscription_id.

2. Why This Architecture

2.0 Foundational principle: open source first, no subscription costs

Hard rule: Vector commits to an open-source-first stack with no per-customer subscription costs to upstream vendors. The order of preference for any external dependency is: (1) MIT/BSD/Apache-licensed open source, (2) self-hosted open core, (3) build our own, (4) paid SaaS as last resort only. This is why Lexical was chosen over Tiptap, Craft.js over BuilderIO, self-hosted Postgres FTS over Algolia. Subscription costs compound forever; one-time build effort amortises.

2.1 Business benefits

  • Infinite extensibility without code: padmins compose their own artefact field schemas. No deploy needed.
  • Subscription tiering by feature: execution layer, strategic layer, testing container as separate SKUs.
  • Compliance as data, not code: healthcare, fintech, govtech ship as field schema packs. One codebase, infinite verticals.
  • Team autonomy at scale: different workspaces within one subscription get different field schemas without forking.

2.2 Architectural benefits

  • Schema is stable — feature growth happens in data, not DDL.
  • Cell-level visibility unifies governance across all artefact types.
  • Search index is uniform — every artefact and field has a searchable text column.
  • Samantha SDK can render any workspace's fields without knowing the DB shape.

3. Three-Table Pattern

Every artefact type follows the same three-table pattern, named consistently with the o_ prefix:

o_artefacts_<domain>_<type>           -- core artefact rows
o_artefacts_<domain>_<type>_schema    -- field definitions per workspace
o_artefacts_<domain>_<type>_field_values  -- per-artefact extended field data

3.1 Core artefact table

The core table holds only the universal fixed column set. Nothing optional or workspace-specific belongs here — it goes in _schema + _field_values.

CREATE TABLE o_artefacts_execution_defects (
    id                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    key_num             BIGINT      NOT NULL,
    title               TEXT        NOT NULL,
    description         TEXT,
    content             JSONB,
    content_plain_text  TEXT,
    owner_id            UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_by          UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    updated_by          UUID        REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at          TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    archived_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
    visibility          SMALLINT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 REFERENCES o_artefact_visibility_levels(level),
    visibility_scope_id UUID,
    search_index        TSVECTOR,

    CONSTRAINT o_de_key_num_positive CHECK (key_num > 0),
    CONSTRAINT o_de_title_nonempty   CHECK (length(btrim(title)) > 0),
    UNIQUE (subscription_id, key_num)
);

3.2 Schema table — field definitions per workspace

One row per field per workspace. The padmin creates these rows in Vector's settings UI. Samantha reads them to know what fields exist, in what order, and what control to render.

CREATE TABLE o_artefacts_execution_defects_schema (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    field_name      TEXT        NOT NULL,
    label           TEXT        NOT NULL,
    type            TEXT        NOT NULL,
    required        BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    position        INTEGER     NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    default_value   TEXT,
    options_json    JSONB,
    config_json     JSONB,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT o_de_schema_field_name_nonempty CHECK (length(btrim(field_name)) > 0),
    CONSTRAINT o_de_schema_type_valid CHECK (
        type IN ('textbox','richtext','integer','decimal','date','boolean','select','multiselect','radio','user','url')
    ),
    UNIQUE (subscription_id, field_name)
);

Key design decisions:

  • UNIQUE (subscription_id, field_name) — enforces one schema definition per field per workspace. Workspace A and B can both define severity — they are separate rows with independent options_json and defaults.
  • type is the renderer selector — Samantha maps it to the correct React control. Adding a new renderer (e.g. starrating) requires only a frontend registry entry, no migration.
  • options_json is a JSONB array — null for non-select types, populated for select/multiselect/radio. Options are workspace-scoped, not global to the field name.
  • config_json holds per-type config — validation rules (min/max, regex), placeholder text, future extension.

3.3 Field values table — extended data per artefact

One row per (artefact, field_name). Typed value columns mirror Jira's customfieldvalue pattern — one column per type, exactly one non-null per row. Multi-value fields (multiselect) produce multiple rows sharing the same artefact_id + field_name.

CREATE TABLE o_artefacts_execution_defects_field_values (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    artefact_id     UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES o_artefacts_execution_defects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    schema_field_id UUID        REFERENCES o_artefacts_execution_defects_schema(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
    field_name      TEXT        NOT NULL,
    string_value    TEXT,
    number_value    NUMERIC(19,4),
    text_value      TEXT,
    date_value      DATE,
    visibility      SMALLINT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 REFERENCES o_artefact_visibility_levels(level),
    visibility_scope_id UUID,
    source_artefact_id  UUID,
    created_by      UUID        NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
    created_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),

    CONSTRAINT o_de_fv_field_name_nonempty CHECK (length(btrim(field_name)) > 0),
    UNIQUE (artefact_id, field_name)
);

Column mapping by type:

typeRenders asValue column
textboxSingle-line inputstring_value
richtextLexical editortext_value
integerNumber input (integer)number_value
decimalNumber input (decimal)number_value
dateDate pickerdate_value
booleanCheckbox / togglestring_value ('true'/'false')
selectDropdown — singlestring_value
multiselectMulti-selectstring_value (one row per value)
radioRadio buttonsstring_value
userUser pickerstring_value (UUID)
urlURL inputstring_value

schema_field_id links back to the schema definition for constraint checking. field_name is denormalised for read performance — queries don't need to join schema to display a value. source_artefact_id records provenance when content is copied from another artefact via search-and-compose.

4. Workspace Isolation in Practice

Workspace A (software team) and Workspace B (marketing team) both use defects. They share the same three tables — isolation is entirely by subscription_id.

4.1 Schema query per workspace

-- Workspace A's field definitions
SELECT field_name, label, type, required, position, options_json
FROM o_artefacts_execution_defects_schema
WHERE subscription_id = 'aaaaaaaa-uuid'
ORDER BY position;

-- Returns:
-- severity | Severity | select | true  | 1 | ["low","medium","high","critical"]
-- browser  | Browser  | select | false | 2 | ["chrome","firefox","safari"]
-- steps    | Steps    | richtext| false | 3 | null

-- Workspace B's field definitions (same table, different subscription)
SELECT field_name, label, type, required, position, options_json
FROM o_artefacts_execution_defects_schema
WHERE subscription_id = 'bbbbbbbb-uuid'
ORDER BY position;

-- Returns:
-- severity | Severity | select  | true  | 1 | ["minor","major","blocker"]
-- campaign | Campaign | textbox | false | 2 | null

4.2 Value query per artefact

-- Values for defect-001 (Workspace A)
SELECT field_name, string_value, number_value, text_value, date_value
FROM o_artefacts_execution_defects_field_values
WHERE artefact_id = 'defect-001-uuid'
  AND subscription_id = 'aaaaaaaa-uuid';

-- Returns:
-- severity | critical | null | null | null
-- browser  | chrome   | null | null | null

4.3 The UNIQUE constraint in action

UNIQUE (subscription_id, field_name) on the schema table means:

  • Workspace A can define severity once — second INSERT fails with a unique violation.
  • Workspace B can also define severity — different subscription_id, different row, independent options list.
  • Neither workspace can accidentally see the other's schema rows — every query filters by subscription_id.

5. Samantha Fields API

The Samantha SDK exposes the three-table pattern to custom app authors through a fields API. App authors never touch the DB directly — they call Samantha, which resolves schema, values, and renderer from a single JSON-pass call.

5.1 Three levels of control

// Level 1 — data only
samantha.fields.getSchema({ artefactType: 'defects' })
// returns [{ fieldName, label, type, required, position, options, defaultValue }, ...]

samantha.fields.getValue({ artefactId: 'uuid', artefactType: 'defects', fieldName: 'severity' })
// returns { value: 'critical', type: 'select', label: 'Severity' }

samantha.fields.setValue({ artefactId: 'uuid', artefactType: 'defects', fieldName: 'severity', value: 'high' })

// Level 2 — schema-aware (app renders own controls)
const schema = await samantha.fields.getSchema({ artefactType: 'defects' })
// app reads schema.type and picks its own component

// Level 3 — full render (Samantha picks the control)
samantha.fields.renderField({
  artefactId: 'uuid-or-null',   // null = new unsaved artefact
  artefactType: 'defects',
  fieldName: 'severity',
  readOnly: false,
  onChange: (val) => {}
})

5.2 JSX shorthand

// Single field — Samantha picks the control from schema type
<Samantha.Field artefactId={item.id} artefactType="defects" fieldName="severity" />

// Read-only display
<Samantha.Field artefactId={item.id} artefactType="defects" fieldName="severity" readOnly />

// Full dynamic form — all fields in position order
<Samantha.FieldGroup artefactId={item.id} artefactType="defects" />

5.3 How type drives rendering

Samantha maintains an internal registry mapping type strings to React components. When renderField is called, it reads the schema row for the requested field, looks up fieldRenderers[row.type], and renders. The padmin changes type = 'radio' instead of 'select' in settings — the app renders radio buttons without any code change. Storage is identical either way.

const fieldRenderers = {
  textbox:     TextboxField,
  richtext:    RichTextField,
  integer:     NumberField,
  decimal:     NumberField,
  date:        DateField,
  boolean:     CheckboxField,
  select:      SelectField,
  multiselect: MultiSelectField,
  radio:       RadioField,
  user:        UserPickerField,
  url:         UrlField,
}

5.4 New artefact staged-write flow

When artefactId is null, Samantha operates in staged-write mode: resolves schema for the artefactType, renders fields with default values, stages writes in memory. On samantha.fields.commitNew({ artefactType, title, stagedValues }) — creates the core artefact row first, then bulk-inserts all staged field value rows. Prevents orphaned value rows for artefacts never saved.

6. Cell-Level Visibility Model

Every field value row carries its own visibility flag. Default policy: private (0) unless explicitly elevated.

FlagLevelWho can see
0PrivateAuthor only
1ProductThe product/vertical silo
2WorkspaceEntire subscription
3TenantCross-subscription (future)
 
-- Fetch values the requesting user can see
SELECT field_name, string_value, text_value, number_value, date_value
FROM o_artefacts_execution_defects_field_values
WHERE artefact_id = $1
  AND subscription_id = $2
  AND visibility <= $user_max_visibility;

7. Core Column Set (universal, every artefact type)

ColumnTypeNotes
idUUID PKDefault gen_random_uuid()
subscription_idUUID NOT NULLWorkspace isolation; FK to subscriptions(id)
key_numBIGINT NOT NULLPer-subscription human-readable sequence. UNIQUE per (subscription_id, key_num).
titleTEXT NOT NULLShort display name. CHECK length > 0.
descriptionTEXTPlain-text tagline. Nullable.
contentJSONBLexical editor document. Nullable.
content_plain_textTEXTStripped version of content for FTS and previews.
owner_idUUID NOT NULLAssignable owner; FK to users(id). Reassignable.
created_byUUID NOT NULLAuthor; FK to users(id). Immutable after insert.
updated_byUUIDLast person to modify the row. Updated on every write.
created_atTIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULLDefault now().
updated_atTIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULLManaged by set_updated_at() trigger.
archived_atTIMESTAMPTZSoft-delete. NULL = active.
visibilitySMALLINT NOT NULLDefault 0. FK to o_artefact_visibility_levels(level).
visibility_scope_idUUIDRequired when visibility >= 1. No FK yet — Phase 2.
search_indexTSVECTORPopulated by outbox worker (R012). GIN-indexed.

8. Phase 1 Artefact Types

Core tableSchema tableField values table
o_artefacts_execution_user_stories..._schema..._field_values
o_artefacts_execution_defects..._schema..._field_values
o_artefacts_execution_tasks..._schema..._field_values
o_artefacts_execution_test_cases..._schema..._field_values
o_artefacts_strategic..._schema..._field_values

5 artefact types x 3 tables each = 15 tables in Phase 1 (plus shared infrastructure: type registry, visibility levels, notes, versions, outbox). o_artefacts_strategic adds hierarchy_parent_id self-referential FK for same-type nesting.

9. Phase 2+ Type Catalogue

Same three-table pattern across all types. All tables carry the o_ prefix. A code generator can scaffold from a YAML manifest.

9.1 Artefacts (additional types)

  • o_artefacts_risk
  • o_artefacts_incidents
  • o_artefacts_scope_change
  • o_artefacts_feedback_user
  • o_artefacts_feedback_client
  • o_artefacts_feedback_stake_holder
  • o_artefacts_technical_requirements
  • o_artefacts_sme
  • o_artefacts_governance_requirements
  • o_artefacts_support_tickets
  • o_artefacts_health_scores

9.2 Facilitation (session records)

  • o_facilitation_session_stand_up
  • o_facilitation_session_retrospective
  • o_facilitation_session_review
  • o_facilitation_session_refinement
  • o_facilitation_session_planning
  • o_facilitation_session_estimation
  • o_facilitation_session_demo
  • o_facilitation_session_technical_spike
  • o_facilitation_session_bug_triage
  • o_facilitation_session_security_review
  • o_facilitation_session_compliance_review
  • o_facilitation_session_post_mortems
  • o_facilitation_session_governance

9.3 Timebox (planning containers)

  • o_timebox_sprints
  • o_timebox_releases
  • o_timebox_pi (Program Increments)
  • o_timebox_milestones
  • Waterfall: o_timebox_waterfall_requirements, o_timebox_waterfall_implementation, o_timebox_waterfall_testing, o_timebox_waterfall_deployment, o_timebox_waterfall_maintenance, o_timebox_waterfall_start_up, o_timebox_waterfall_initiation, o_timebox_waterfall_stage_delivery, o_timebox_waterfall_product_delivery, o_timebox_waterfall_closing_out

9.4 Testing containers

  • o_testing_test_case
  • o_testing_test_type
  • o_testing_test_group
  • o_testing_test_plan
  • o_testing_test_profile

9.5 Phase 2 totals

~40 additional artefact types x 3 tables each = ~120 tables. Adding a new type = one row in o_artefact_type_registry + three new tables. No special-case logic.

10. Search Strategy

10.1 Hybrid lexical + semantic (Phase 1)

Every core table has search_index TSVECTOR (GIN-indexed). Every _field_values table gains a content_embedding vector(768) column (HNSW index, WHERE archived_at IS NULL). The outbox worker (R012) maintains both asynchronously. Search bar fuses lexical and semantic scores via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

10.2 Outbox pattern (R012)

Triggers queue (artefact_type, artefact_id) to o_search_index_outbox on every write. A Go worker claims rows with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, recomputes TSVECTOR, fetches embedding from self-hosted Ollama, updates both columns, deletes the outbox row. At-least-once, self-healing across restarts.

11. Shared Infrastructure Tables

  • o_artefact_type_registry — scope_key PK, artefact_table, default_prefix, display_label, phase. Seeded by migration 059.
  • o_subscription_artefact_type_overrides — padmin-mutable display prefix per workspace.
  • o_artefact_visibility_levels — 4 rows: (0, private), (1, product), (2, workspace), (3, tenant).
  • o_artefact_notes — append-only threaded notes, polymorphic on (artefact_type, artefact_id).
  • o_artefact_note_reads — unread tracking per (user, artefact_type, artefact_id).
  • o_artefact_versions — snapshot-based undo (Q10 Option D). snapshot_jsonb contains full artefact + field values state.
  • o_search_index_outbox — durable outbox for async TSVECTOR + embedding worker.

12. Migration Path

12.1 Current state (migrations 049–059)

Migrations 049–059 created the infrastructure and a four-table pattern (core + _template_forms + _template_form_fields + _field_values). The design was validated and then superseded by the three-table pattern documented here. The next migration batch will DROP the _template_forms and _template_form_fields tables, CREATE the _schema tables in their place, and update _field_values to use typed columns and schema_field_id.

12.2 Old portfolio/user-story tables

Tables portfolio_items, portfolio_item_types, item_field_definitions, item_field_values, item_field_options remain untouched. Existing pages and APIs continue working. Cutover happens after the new tables are validated.

12.3 Cutover sequence

  1. Migrate existing portfolio_items rows to o_artefacts_strategic + _field_values.
  2. Migrate user_stories rows to o_artefacts_execution_user_stories + _field_values.
  3. Update API routes to read from new tables.
  4. Drop old pages, then old tables.

13. Backend API Patterns

GET /api/artefacts/execution/defects/:id
-- Returns: { id, key_num, title, description, owner_id, ...,
--   fields: [{ field_name, label, type, value, visibility }],  // visibility-filtered
--   schema: [{ field_name, label, type, required, position, options }] }

GET /api/artefacts/search?q=login+flow&types=defects,user_stories
-- Returns: [{ artefact_id, type, title, field_name, snippet, visibility }]
-- Visibility-filtered, subscription-scoped

POST /api/artefacts/execution/defects/bulk-apply
-- Body: { artefact_ids, field_name, value, source_artefact_id, visibility }
-- Atomic batch insert/update

14. Open Questions

  • visibility_scope_id semantics: which entity does it reference? Products or workspaces table — not yet designed. Phase 2.
  • Effective user visibility computation: how is user_max_visibility derived? Likely user.role + product_membership + subscription.policy.
  • Cross-artefact links: polymorphic artefact_links table vs typed FK on core table. Recommend polymorphic for consistency.
  • Computed/rollup fields: count_child_*, rollup aggregates — materialised views or stored field values?
  • Validation rules on schema: min/max for integers, regex for textbox — likely config_json keys, not separate columns.
  • Conditional field visibility: show field X only if field Y = 'critical' — Phase 2.

15. Sources

  1. R007 — Strategic item field catalogue
  2. R008 — Execution item field catalogue
  3. R009 — Strategic vs execution merged comparison
  4. R012 — pgvector and outbox pattern validation
  5. R013 — Jira custom fields architecture and scoping
  6. R014 — Samantha SDK fields API surface design
  7. Conversation 2026-04-30 — three-table pattern finalised, workspace isolation confirmed, type-as-renderer-selector confirmed, JSON-pass API confirmed
